reasoning.issue: The question asks what test the build-up (Cumulative Amount) basket is based on and when it starts accruing capacity.
reasoning.analysis:
The Cumulative Amount basket is defined as the sum (without duplication) of multiple additive sources, computed from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs (growth_start_anchor: closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start; start_date_language: 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs').

The basket has two structural layers:

1. A 'greatest of' three-test aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) that feeds into the top-level sum. The three tests, each measured cumulatively since the start anchor, are:
   - 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (6e76ed06_builder_source_cni; cap_grower_pct: 50%, cap_grower_reference: consolidated_net_income)
   - The Available Retained ECF Amount — cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) or other debt under Section 2.10(h), floored at $0 per fiscal year (6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf)
   - 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended LTM period (6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc; cap_grower_pct: 140%, cap_grower_reference: consolidated_ebitda_ltm)
   The greatest of these three amounts is selected (aggregation_function: greatest_of).

2. Additional additive sources summed on top of the greatest-of result:
   - Starter amount: greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM (6e76ed06_builder_source_starter; cap_usd: $130M, cap_grower_pct: 100%, starter_uses_greater_of: true)
   - 100% of equity proceeds received since the start date (6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds; cap_grower_pct: 100%, cap_grower_reference: equity_proceeds_usd)
   - Retained asset sale proceeds not swept under the mandatory prepayment provisions (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds; flows in via proceeds_flow from asset_sale_event)
   - Investment returns (6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns)
   - Debt-to-equity conversion proceeds (6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion)

The basket is reduced by usage for restricted payments, restricted debt payments, and investments (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage all true), but is NOT reduced by unrestricted subsidiary designations (reduced_by_designation: false).

The basket is also shared with the builder RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)), subject to intercreditor agreement constraints.
reasoning.interactions: 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate (greatest_of CNI/ECF/EBITDA-FC) contributes to 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission at child_index 0, then the remaining additive sources (starter, equity proceeds, asset sale proceeds, investment returns, debt conversion) each contribute additively. The overall basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) is shared with the RDP builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission / Section 6.09(a)(A)), meaning usage under either covenant draws from the same pool. Retained asset sale proceeds flow into the basket via the asset_sale_event proceeds_flow edge (retained_after_sweep), meaning only proceeds not swept under the leverage-based prepayment tiers enter the builder basket.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is a sum-of-sources structure anchored to the first day of the fiscal quarter containing the Closing Date; its core growth driver is the greatest of (i) 50% of cumulative CNI, (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount, or (iii) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus additive sources including a $130M/100% EBITDA starter, 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion amounts.

stage2.answer: The build-up basket, defined as the 'Cumulative Amount,' begins accruing on the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. Its core growth engine is the greatest of three tests measured cumulatively since that start date: (i) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_cni], (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to mandatory prepayments, floored at $0) [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf], or (iii) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc]. To that greatest-of amount, the basket adds: a starter of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_starter], 100% of equity proceeds received since closing [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds], retained asset sale proceeds not mandatorily swept [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds], investment returns [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns], and debt-to-equity conversion amounts [norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion]; the basket is reduced dollar-for-dollar by usage for restricted payments, restricted debt payments, and investments, and is shared with the restricted debt payment builder basket under Section 6.09(a)(A) [norm 6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission].

citations.quote: "Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
Starter: greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM; growth_start_anchor: closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs
Available Retained ECF Amount: portion of Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans pursuant to Section 2.10(f)... in no event shall... be less than $0
140% of Consolidated EBITDA LTM; cumulative since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
Greatest of CNI source, ECF source, and EBITDA-FC source aggregated via greatest_of function
100% of equity proceeds received since the closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
Retained asset sale proceeds (net of mandatory sweep) additive to Cumulative Amount since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
Investment returns additive to Cumulative Amount since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
Debt-to-equity conversion amounts additive to Cumulative Amount since closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start
to the extent not prohibited by any applicable Intercreditor Agreement... with the Cumulative Amount

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries to its equity holders or up the corporate chain.
reasoning.analysis: Section 6.06(p) contains a categorical, unconditional permission (norm_id: 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission) authorizing 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The v3 entity attributes confirm this basket is categorical (is_categorical: true), has no dollar cap (capacity_category: 'categorical', capacity_composition: 'n_a'), does not require a valid designation at the time of distribution (requires_valid_designation: false), and partial_applicability is false (meaning it applies in full without conditions). The basket also covers proceeds of such equity interests (covers_proceeds: true) and non-cash distributions at fair market value (includes_noncash_at_fmv: true). Notably, the basket rebuilds the builder basket (rebuilds_builder_basket: true) and distributions can cascade (distributions_can_cascade: true), meaning distributions from an Unrestricted Subsidiary up through the corporate chain are expressly contemplated. No defeaters are listed against this norm. The J.Crew blocker (norm_id: 6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) restricts designation of Unrestricted Subsidiaries that own Material IP, but does not restrict distributions of equity interests in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries. The supplementary general RP basket at Section 6.06(j) provides an alternative capped pathway for dividends generally, but it is not needed here given the categorical permission at 6.06(p).
reasoning.interactions: The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) operates at the designation stage, not the distribution stage, and does not defeat the 6.06(p) permission. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) at Section 6.06(j) is a separate, capped pathway; the 6.06(p) categorical basket is the more directly applicable provision. No proceeds-flow or contributes_to interactions limit the 6.06(p) permission.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, the Borrower is categorically and unconditionally permitted under Section 6.06(p) to distribute, by dividend or otherwise, equity interests (and indebtedness, assets, or proceeds thereof) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, without a dollar cap or financial maintenance condition.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) contains a categorical, uncapped permission for 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof' [Section 6.06(p), p.198]. This basket imposes no dollar limit, no pro forma leverage test, and no requirement that the relevant subsidiary remain validly designated at the time of distribution; non-cash distributions are valued at fair market value and proceeds of such equity interests are also covered. The J.Crew blocker applies only to the designation of Unrestricted Subsidiaries (prohibiting designation if the subsidiary would own Material IP), not to distributions of equity already held in an Unrestricted Subsidiary [Section Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary, p.89].

citations.quote: the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof

no Restricted Subsidiary may be designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary if, immediately after giving effect to such designation, such Unrestricted Subsidiary would own or have an exclusive license...to any Material Intellectual Property.

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(p) the Borrower can dividend shares of Equity Interest or any assets of an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

reasoning.issue: Whether any investment, restricted debt payment (RDP), or other baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends under the credit agreement.
reasoning.analysis:
The question focuses on reallocation mechanics — specifically whether capacity from investment baskets or RDP baskets can flow into restricted payment capacity.

1. **General RP Basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission)**: The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) permits dividends up to the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. Its `action_scope` is 'reallocable', and `capacity_composition` is 'fungible'. This basket is available for direct RP use and is listed as reallocable.

2. **General RDP Basket (6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission)**: Under Section 6.09(a)(I), the general RDP basket permits debt prepayments up to the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. Its `action_scope` is also 'reallocable'. Crucially, investment pathway entity 6e76ed06_rp_investment_pathway_3 (Section 6.03(y)) explicitly references this basket by adding 'the aggregate total of all amounts available to be utilized for Restricted Debt Payments pursuant to Section 6.09(a)' to the investment basket cap — confirming that RDP capacity can be reallocated into the general investment basket. The inverse direction (RDP basket → RP) is not expressly stated, but the general RDP basket and general RP basket share the same dollar cap ($130M / 100% EBITDA) and both have `action_scope: reallocable`, indicating the caps are fungible across covenants.

3. **Builder/Cumulative Amount Basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission / 6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission)**: The Cumulative Amount (builder basket) under the Definition of Cumulative Amount / Section 6.09(a)(A) is explicitly cross-covenant: it covers `make_dividend_payment`, `repurchase_equity`, `pay_subordinated_debt`, and `make_investment` within a single shared pool. The `builder_rdp_basket` (Section 6.09(a)(A)) has `shares_with_rp_builder: true`, confirming the Cumulative Amount is shared between the RP builder basket and RDP builder basket — usage under one reduces availability for the other. The v3 builder_basket entity confirms `reduced_by_rp_usage: true`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage: true`, and `reduced_by_investment_usage: true`. Investment pathway Section 6.03(x) also draws on the Cumulative Amount, further confirming the shared pool.

4. **Investment Pathway 6.03(y)**: Explicitly absorbs RDP capacity — the investment cap is the greater of $130M/100% EBITDA PLUS all amounts available under Section 6.09(a). This is a one-way reallocation (RDP → Investment), not necessarily the reverse.

In summary: (a) the Cumulative Amount basket is fully shared across RPs, RDPs, and investments; (b) the general RDP basket and general RP basket share the same dollar cap and are both flagged `reallocable`; (c) the general RDP basket capacity feeds directly into the general investment basket under Section 6.03(y).
reasoning.interactions: The builder_rdp_basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission) shares capacity with the RP builder basket via `shares_with_rp_builder: true` — both draw on the same Cumulative Amount pool (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission). The builder basket is reduced by RP, RDP, and investment usage (`reduced_by_rp_usage`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage`, `reduced_by_investment_usage` all true). Investment pathway 6.03(y) absorbs RDP basket capacity additively. The general RP basket and general RDP basket each independently cap at $130M/100% EBITDA but are both flagged reallocable, suggesting fungibility across those covenant silos.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is explicitly shared across restricted payments, RDP, and investments in a single pool; the general RDP basket ($130M/100% EBITDA) is flagged reallocable; and investment basket Section 6.03(y) expressly absorbs available RDP capacity, with the builder RDP basket sharing capacity with the RP builder basket.

stage2.answer: Yes, multiple baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends. First, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] is a single shared pool that covers restricted payments, RDP, and investments simultaneously — usage under any one reduces the others, and the builder RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(A) [p.199] expressly shares this pool with the RP builder basket (`shares_with_rp_builder: true`). Second, the general RDP basket (greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA) under Section 6.09(a)(I) [p.200] has `action_scope: reallocable`, consistent with the matching general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) [p.196], indicating fungibility at the same dollar cap across the two covenants. Third, the general investment basket under Section 6.03(y) expressly incorporates 'the aggregate total of all amounts available to be utilized for Restricted Debt Payments pursuant to Section 6.09(a)' as additive investment capacity, confirming that RDP capacity can be reallocated into investments. The builder RDP basket is subject to applicable intercreditor agreements and subordination terms [Section 6.09(a)(A), p.199].

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(j) amount available for Restricted Debt Payment under 6.09(a) and amounts available for Investments under 6.03(y) can be reallocated to the making of Dividends. 6.09(a) includes the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. 6.09(a) also includes other more tailored baskets available for Restricted Debt Payments which may or may not be available for reallocation, including intercompany debt payments and payments in connection with a reorganization or IPO.

reasoning.issue: Whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividends, and if so, through which mechanism(s) and subject to what conditions.
reasoning.analysis:
Three pathways are relevant:

1. **Builder Basket / Cumulative Amount (primary mechanism for asset sale proceeds):** Norm `6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds` is a builder source that contributes to the Cumulative Amount (`6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission`). The proceeds_flow confirms that Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales subject to mandatory prepayment under Section 2.10(c) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount when *retained* — i.e., the portion not swept to prepay term loans per the leverage-based sweep tiers. The sweep tiers (provision_level_entities) show: 100% sweep if First Lien Leverage Ratio >5.75x; 50% sweep if >5.50x but ≤5.75x; 0% sweep if ≤5.50x. Only the retained (non-swept) portion enters the Cumulative Amount. Once in the Cumulative Amount, `6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission` permits dividends (among other uses), with the basket computed as the greatest of multiple sources. The builder basket is reduced by RP, RDP, and investment usage (no duplication).

2. **General RP Basket (Section 6.06(j)):** Norm `6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission` permits dividends up to the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. This basket is fungible and reallocable — it is not sourced specifically from asset sale proceeds, but because it is a general-purpose, fungible basket, asset sale proceeds (as cash) can fund dividends drawn under it. The basket is not conditioned on leverage.

3. **Ratio Basket (Section 6.06(o)):** Unlimited dividends are permitted on a Pro Forma Basis if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤5.75x (or no worse than closing ratio). Asset sale proceeds could fund dividends under this basket if the leverage test is satisfied.

The asset-sale-specific pathway is through the retained proceeds component of the Cumulative Amount. The general RP basket and ratio basket are available as parallel paths funded by any cash (including asset sale proceeds), subject to their respective caps/conditions.
reasoning.interactions: The `6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds` norm contributes (via sum aggregation) to `6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission` (the Cumulative Amount), which in turn permits dividends. The sweep tier entities gate how much of asset sale proceeds enter the Cumulative Amount — at leverage >5.75x, 100% must be swept to prepay debt, leaving nothing for the builder basket from that source. The general RP basket (`6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission`) and ratio basket operate independently and are not gated by the sweep mechanics. Builder basket usage reduces available capacity and is not duplicated across RP, RDP, and investment uses.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends: (i) the retained (non-swept) portion of asset sale Net Cash Proceeds builds the Cumulative Amount and may be distributed as dividends; and (ii) asset sale cash proceeds may fund dividends under the standalone general RP basket (up to the greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA) or the ratio basket (unlimited, subject to First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤5.75x pro forma).

stage2.answer: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends through multiple pathways. First, Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales that are retained after the leverage-based mandatory prepayment sweep (100% sweep if First Lien Leverage Ratio >5.75x; 50% if >5.50x–≤5.75x; 0% if ≤5.50x) flow into the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) and may be used for dividends under that basket [Definition of Cumulative Amount / Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage, p. 32]. Second, asset sale cash proceeds may fund dividends under the general RP basket up to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (this basket is fungible and not source-specific) [Section 6.06(j), p. 196]. Third, unlimited dividends are available if, on a Pro Forma Basis, First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤5.75x (or no worse than the closing ratio) [Section 6.06(o), p. 198]. The builder basket is reduced dollar-for-dollar by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage to prevent double-counting.

citations.quote: "Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
"Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
additional Dividends may be made...in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period
unlimited additional Dividends; provided that on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio...shall be no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75 to 1.00

questions.gold_answer: Yes, Retained Asset Sale Proceeds build the Cumulative Amount which consists of proceeds from: Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales not subject to prepayment on account of Section 2.10(c)(iv), permitting proceeds from any Asset Sale using the unlimited basket 6.05(z) if such Asset Sale is a sale of a product line and the pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.25x or less or if such test is no worse pro forma. Also includes asset sale proceeds not swept when First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 5.75x or less (50% of proceeds) or 5.50x or less (100% of proceeds). Also includes Net Cash Proceeds from non-collateral assets, ordinary course asset sales, asset sales from non-ratio baskets, casualty events, and proceeds from collateral assets below de minimis thresholds of $20M/15% EBITDA individual and $40M/30% EBITDA annual.

reasoning.issue: The question asks for the total quantifiable dividend capacity across all applicable baskets in the restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis:
Step 1 — Classify each basket by capacity_category per synthesis guidance rules:

(A) GENERAL_PURPOSE baskets (summed independently unless fungible/shared):
• 6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission [Section 6.06(j), p.196]: greater of $130M or 100% Consolidated EBITDA (LTM). capacity_composition = 'fungible'. This is a standalone general-purpose RP basket.
• 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]: The Cumulative Amount, which is computed from multiple sources: (i) a starter of greater of $130M or 100% EBITDA (one-time, at closing quarter start); plus (ii) the greatest of 50% CNI, Available Retained ECF, or 140% EBITDA-FC (cumulative since closing); plus (iii) 100% of equity proceeds; plus (iv) retained asset sale proceeds; plus (v) investment returns; plus (vi) debt conversions. The builder basket is a separate general_purpose basket from the general RP basket (no shares_capacity_pool link identified between them). Dollar floor at inception = greater of $130M or 100% EBITDA.
• 6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission [Section 6.09(a)(I), p.200]: greater of $130M or 100% Consolidated EBITDA. This is an RDP basket (separate covenant hierarchy) classified general_purpose. However, it is the RDP general basket — its capacity is for restricted debt payments, not dividends directly. It is reallocable (action_scope = 'reallocable') and may be used for RP purposes via cross-covenant reallocation, but is architecturally separate from the RP baskets.

Step 2 — No shares_capacity_pool link exists between the general_rp_basket and the builder_basket, so they are independent capacity pools.

Step 3 — RESTRICTED_PURPOSE baskets (listed separately, not summed into general total):
• 6e76ed06_management_equity_basket_permission [Section 6.06(b), p.195]: greater of $20M or 15% EBITDA per annum (with carryforward). Restricted to management equity repurchases only.

Step 4 — UNLIMITED_CONDITIONAL baskets (listed separately):
• 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission [Section 6.06(o), p.198]: Unlimited dividends if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x on a pro forma basis OR no-worse test met. Not quantifiable to a dollar amount absent leverage data.

Step 5 — CATEGORICAL baskets (no dollar cap, listed separately):
• Tax distributions [6.06(c)], holdco overhead [6.06(k)], equity award repurchases [6.06(d)], unrestricted sub distributions [6.06(p)].

Step 6 — QUANTIFIABLE GENERAL-PURPOSE TOTAL (dollar floors only, treating EBITDA grower as variable):
• General RP basket floor: $130,000,000
• Builder basket floor (starter component alone): $130,000,000
• Builder basket additional sources (CNI, ECF, EBITDA-FC, equity proceeds, etc.) are variable/accrued — not quantifiable at closing without actual figures.
• General RDP basket floor (reallocable): $130,000,000 — but note this is a separate RDP covenant basket; inclusion in RP dividend capacity depends on reallocation mechanics.

General-purpose RP dividend capacity (dollar floors, excluding variable builder accruals and RDP reallocation): $130M (general RP basket) + builder basket (floor $130M at closing, growing thereafter) = at minimum $260M in dollar floors across the two independent general-purpose pools, before EBITDA growers and cumulative builder accruals.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) is reduced by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage=true, reduced_by_rdp_usage=true, reduced_by_investment_usage=true). The general RDP basket (6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission) is architecturally separate (RDP covenant) but action_scope='reallocable', meaning its capacity can cross into RP use. The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) provides unlimited capacity if leverage conditions are met but is not a fixed dollar figure. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: The quantifiable dividend capacity consists of two independent general-purpose pools with dollar floors totaling at least $260M (each at the greater of $130M or 100% Consolidated EBITDA), plus a restricted-purpose management equity basket of the greater of $20M or 15% EBITDA per annum; unlimited capacity is also available under the ratio basket if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma.

stage2.answer: The quantifiable dividend capacity comprises: (1) the General RP Basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196] — an independent general-purpose pool capped at the greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period; (2) the Cumulative Amount (Builder Basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] — a separate general-purpose pool starting at the greater of $130,000,000 or 100% EBITDA (at the first day of the closing fiscal quarter) and growing by the greatest of 50% CNI, Available Retained ECF, or 140% EBITDA-FC (cumulative since closing), plus 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversions, reduced by prior RP/RDP/investment usage; and (3) the Management Equity Basket [Section 6.06(b), p.195] — a restricted-purpose basket (equity repurchases from management only) capped at the greater of $20,000,000 or 15% of Consolidated EBITDA per annum (with carryforward). The two general-purpose pools are independent (no shared-pool link), yielding combined dollar floors of at least $260,000,000 before EBITDA growers and builder accruals apply; the management basket adds up to $20M+ (restricted use). An unlimited dividend basket is also available under [Section 6.06(o), p.198] if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x pro forma (or no-worse), but this is not a fixed dollar amount. The General RDP Basket [Section 6.09(a)(I), p.200] (greater of $130M or 100% EBITDA) is reallocable and may supplement dividend capacity cross-covenant.

General RP basket: $130m/100% EBITDA. General prepayment of debt basket: $130m/100% EBITDA. General investment basket: $130m/100% EBITDA.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division with negative EBITDA to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, focusing on whether any RP basket permits this transaction.
reasoning.analysis: The primary basket to analyze is the ratio RP basket under Section 6.06(o) (norm: 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission), which permits unlimited dividends provided the First Lien Leverage Ratio on a Pro Forma Basis is no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x and (y) the First Lien Leverage Ratio WITHOUT giving effect to the transaction (the 'no-worse test'). The absolute threshold test (5.75x) is not satisfied because 6.0x > 5.75x. However, the no-worse test (condition predicate: pro_forma_no_worse|||first_lien_net_leverage) must also be analyzed. The business division has NEGATIVE EBITDA. Removing a negative-EBITDA asset from the consolidated group on a pro forma basis INCREASES (improves) Consolidated EBITDA (the denominator grows), while the numerator (First Lien Net Debt) remains unchanged or decreases. This means the pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio AFTER the dividend is LOWER than the pre-transaction ratio of 6.0x. The no-worse test asks whether the pro forma ratio is no greater than the ratio 'determined without giving effect to the Subject Transactions' — i.e., 6.0x. Since pro forma leverage < 6.0x (due to removal of negative-EBITDA drag), the pro forma ratio will be ≤ 6.0x, and the no-worse test IS satisfied. The condition_tree is structured as an OR — either the absolute 5.75x test OR the no-worse test must be satisfied. Since the no-worse test is met, the ratio basket is available and provides unlimited capacity. The norm's scope explicitly includes 'business_division' as a norm_scopes_object target, confirming coverage of this transaction type. The general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) provides a separate $130m floor (the asset is worth $200m, so this basket alone is insufficient, but it is supplementary). The unsub distribution basket (Section 6.06(p)) is categorical but requires the asset/division to be in an Unrestricted Subsidiary — not directly applicable here as a standalone path unless prior designation occurred.
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) has an OR condition tree: either the 5.75x absolute threshold OR the no-worse test must be satisfied. The absolute test fails at 6.0x, but the no-worse test passes because removing negative-EBITDA improves the ratio pro forma. No defeaters are listed. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) provides an independent $130m capacity (insufficient alone for a $200m asset). The proceeds flow edge to the builder basket is not directly relevant here as this is a dividend, not an asset sale generating cash proceeds to the credit group.
reasoning.interactions_note: The no-worse test in the ratio basket condition_tree is the load-bearing condition that enables this transaction.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, the Borrower can dividend the asset/business division to shareholders at a 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, because the pro forma no-worse test in Section 6.06(o) is satisfied: removing a negative-EBITDA asset improves the consolidated leverage ratio below 6.0x on a pro forma basis, satisfying the OR condition in the ratio basket even though the absolute 5.75x threshold is not met.

stage2.answer: Yes. The Borrower can dividend the $200m business division to shareholders at 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio by relying on the unlimited ratio RP basket under Section 6.06(o) [p. 198]. That basket permits unlimited dividends if, on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x (absolute threshold — not met at 6.0x) or (y) the pre-transaction First Lien Leverage Ratio determined WITHOUT giving effect to the dividend (the 'no-worse test'). Because the divested division carries NEGATIVE EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group on a pro forma basis INCREASES the consolidated EBITDA denominator, causing the pro forma leverage ratio to fall below the pre-transaction 6.0x level — thereby satisfying the no-worse test. Since the condition tree is structured as an OR, satisfying the no-worse test alone is sufficient, and the ratio basket provides unlimited capacity for this transaction. The norm explicitly covers 'business_division' as a permissible object. Separately, the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) [p. 196] provides an independent $130m floor (insufficient alone for a $200m asset but available for any portion), and both analyses are subject to no active defeaters in the data.

citations.quote: unlimited additional Dividends; provided that on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio...shall be no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75 to 1.00 and (y) the First Lien Leverage Ratio (determined without giving effect to the Subject Transactions on a Pro Forma Basis)
additional Dividends may be made...in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period
(noted; not load-bearing for this question) the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of...or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof

questions.gold_answer: Yes, because the Ratio RP basket 6.06(o) permits such transaction as long as the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, even if above 5.75x, is no worse.
